story.iWant: password hashing utility for test data
story.soThat: seeded users have proper hashed passwords for authentication

task.name: Task 1: Create hash-password utility
task.description: Create prisma/utils/hash-password.ts utility file with bcrypt implementation

subtasks.subtask: Create prisma/utils directory if not exists
Add TypeScript types and error handling
Export utility for use in seeders

task.name: Task 2: Update UserSeeder with hash utility
task.description: Integrate hashPassword utility into UserSeeder for all user types

subtasks.subtask: Verify no plain text passwords remain

task.name: Task 3: Testing and validation
task.description: Test hashPassword utility and validate seeded users

subtasks.subtask: Test hashPassword utility with sample passwords
Verify seeded users have hashed passwords in database
Run full seeding and validate password hashing
Document password formats in code comments

details.item: Update UserSeeder to use hashPassword utility
Ensure all seeded users have hashed passwords
Verify no plain text passwords in database
Full type safety for hashPassword function
Compatible with Prisma User model password field
Proper error handling for invalid inputs

doc.title: Epic 13: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure
doc.section: Story 13.6: Hash Password Utility & Test Data Management
doc.snippet: Story 13.6: Hash Password Utility & Test Data Management. As a developer, I want password hashing utility for test data, so that seeded users have proper hashed passwords for authentication. prisma/utils/hash-password.ts utility, bcrypt saltRounds: 10, test passwords: Admin123!, Staff123!, User123!

doc.title: Epic 13 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Password Hashing: bcrypt with saltRounds: 10. No Plain Text: All passwords hashed before seeding. Environment Variables: DATABASE_URL from .env. Idempotent: upsert() prevents duplicate seeding.

doc.title: Story 13-5: Environment-Specific Data & Transactional Seeding
doc.section: Dev Notes - Learnings from Previous Story
doc.snippet: Environment Config Established: Story 13-5 created prisma/config/environment.config.ts with environment-specific settings. Transaction Safety: All seeding operations wrapped in $transaction() from Story 13-5. UserSeeder location: prisma/seeders/user.seeder.ts (existing from Story 13-3).

doc.title: Story 13-3: Individual Module Seeders
doc.snippet: UserSeeder (prisma/seeders/user.seeder.ts): Admin user, staff users, regular users. Hash password utility: import { hashPassword } from '../utils/hash-password'. Sample SMS logs: OTP codes, delivery statuses. Turkish phone numbers (05xx format).

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Utilities: 100% (pure functions, no external dependencies). Services: 80%+ (core business logic). Controllers: 70%+ (HTTP layer, integration with services). Repositories: 70%+ (data access, query logic). Test patterns for hashPassword utility should follow Arrange-Act-Assert.

artifact.reason: Existing UserSeeder class that needs to be enhanced with password hashing. Contains admin user, staff users, and regular user seeding logic.
UserFactory.generate() and generateMany() methods used to create user data. Needs to be integrated with hashPassword utility.
Environment configuration from Story 13-5 that defines data volumes for dev/test/staging. User counts: 50/5/25 users respectively.
Main seed entry point with $transaction wrapper from Story 13-5. Calls all seeders including UserSeeder.

constraints.constraint: All passwords must be hashed using bcrypt with saltRounds: 10 before database storage. No plain text passwords allowed in database.
Utility function pattern: Simple API - input string, output Promise. Reusable across all seeding operations.
Must maintain transaction integrity with $transaction() wrapper from Story 13-5. All seeders run atomically.
All seeding operations must use upsert() or createMany(skipDuplicates: true) to ensure safe re-execution.
Full TypeScript strict mode compliance (Epic 12.4). Type safety required for hashPassword function parameters and return values.
Environment detection via NODE_ENV from Story 13-4. Password formats consistent across all environments (dev/test/staging).

interface.name: hashPassword
interface.description: Password hashing utility using bcrypt. Input: plain text password. Output: Promise resolving to hashed password string with saltRounds: 10.

interface.name: UserSeeder.seed
interface.description: Enhanced UserSeeder method that integrates hashPassword utility for all user types (admin, staff, regular).

tests.standards: Jest unit testing pattern for utilities following Arrange-Act-Assert. Utility tests: 100% coverage required (pure function, no external dependencies). Test bcrypt hashing with various inputs. Integration tests verify seeded users have properly hashed passwords in database.

locations.location: test/utils/ - Unit tests for hashPassword utility
test/seeders/ - Integration tests for UserSeeder with password hashing
test/seed.ts - E2E tests for full seeding process

ideas.idea: Test hashPassword utility with various password inputs (valid, invalid, edge cases). Verify Promise return type and bcrypt hash generation.
Integration test: Run UserSeeder.seed() and verify all created users have hashed passwords (not plain text). Test admin, staff, and regular users separately.
E2E test: Run full seeding (seed.ts main()) and verify database contains only hashed passwords. Test idempotency - running seed twice should not create duplicate or broken passwords.
TypeScript test: Verify hashPassword function type signatures compile correctly. Ensure compatibility with Prisma User model password field type.
